message:
test: cover auth flow, project canonicalization, lock scoping, guard truthy parsing, and threadpool offload paths

Sweep of test additions and updates that pin the new behavior in the
preceding three commits — config/db/guard support, storage/http/app
project lifecycle, and CLI registration-token auth. Grouped into one
test commit because most files cross more than one of those boundaries.

## Auth + registration tokens (cli, http, server)

- `tests/test_cli.py`, `tests/test_cli_extended.py`,
  `tests/test_cli_mail_commands.py`, `tests/test_am_run_basic.py`:
  cover the new `_parse_jsonrpc_response` helper, the structured-content
  unwrapping fix (present-but-non-dict no longer collapses), the
  `_lookup_agent_registration_token` and
  `_lookup_product_registration_token` resolvers (single-token success
  + ambiguous multi-token returns None), `_resolve_local_product_agents`
  (success and "product not found" raise), and
  `_require_cli_product_auth` (explicit token wins over local lookup,
  no-token raises with the documented hint).
  `am-run` tests verify the auto-token loader injects
  `AGENT_MAIL_REGISTRATION_TOKEN` into the spawned subprocess only
  when one unambiguous token can be resolved.

- `tests/test_http_auth.py`: covers `_localhost_bypass_allowed`
  (allow_localhost=False ignores 127.0.0.1, allow_localhost=True
  trusts loopback, IPv6 loopback, and unix-socket peer connections),
  plus the new `_open_existing_project_archive` read-only path
  (returns None for missing storage root, missing `.git`, missing
  project subtree; returns a usable ProjectArchive otherwise).

- `tests/test_http_server.py`, `tests/test_http_transport.py`,
  `tests/test_http_rate_limiting_comprehensive.py`,
  `tests/test_http_redis_rate_limit.py`: thread the new auth args
  through the existing transport / rate-limit cases so they keep
  passing under the auth-required code paths and add coverage for
  the threadpool-offloaded retention/archive-guide collectors
  (`_collect_retention_quota_report_sync`, `_collect_archive_guide_stats_sync`).

## Project canonicalization + identity (app, server, identity)

- `tests/test_identity.py`, `tests/test_identity_resources.py`:
  exercise `_canonicalize_project_identifier` against absolute paths,
  `~` expansion, relative paths joined against the new
  `_project_lookup_base_dir`, plain (non-path) identifiers (returned
  as-is), and worktree symlinks (PWD-vs-cwd-samefile case). Pin that
  symlinked worktrees keep their per-worktree identity in the
  database instead of being collapsed to the underlying repo.

- `tests/test_project_agent_setup.py`: covers the auto-canonicalize
  path for `register_agent` and friends and the new
  `_resolve_doctor_project` resolver (slug, human key, filesystem
  path all resolve to the same Project row).

- `tests/test_server.py`, `tests/test_more_resources.py`: confirm
  the renamed `resource://tooling/projects` resource serves the same
  payload as the old `resource://projects` location.

- `tests/test_archive_browser.py`: read-only browse of an existing
  project archive uses the new `_open_existing_project_archive`
  helper and does NOT mint a fresh archive for a non-existent
  project (regression: a stray browse used to create empty
  `projects/<slug>` directories on disk).

## Storage + product bus (storage, lock scoping, backup uniqueness)

- `tests/test_storage_edges.py` (large addition): covers
  `_sanitize_backup_reason` (slug rules + empty fallback),
  `_create_unique_backup_dir` (timestamp collision loop produces
  distinct directories), `_restore_bundle_into_archive` (in-place
  bundle is staged through a temp dir; existing target is snapshotted
  to `.pre-restore`; `clear_repo_cache` is invoked), and the
  `collect_lock_status(project_slug=...)` / `heal_archive_locks(project_slug=...)`
  scoping (operations on one slug do not see another slug's locks).
  Adds backup-manifest parser tests for `_parse_backup_manifest`,
  `_resolve_backup_artifact_path`, `_resolve_backup_file_artifact`
  including the path-escape rejection case.

- `tests/test_product_bus.py`: extended product-bus coverage that
  exercises the new auth-aware product commands end-to-end through
  the in-process server.

## Guard + worktree integration

- `tests/test_guard_edges.py`, `tests/test_guard_worktrees.py`,
  `tests/test_precommit_enforcement.py`,
  `tests/test_prepush_enforcement_comprehensive.py`,
  `tests/integration/test_worktrees_functionality_e2e.py`:
  pin the new `TRUTHY` parsing in `render_precommit_script` /
  `render_prepush_script` (the literal "0" no longer accidentally
  passes the gate; "true"/"yes"/"y"/"t" still pass). Also cover the
  Windows shim cleanup in `uninstall_guard`: `_agent_mail_shims`
  identifies cmd/ps1 shims by exact-content signature and removes
  them in all three uninstall branches (legacy hook, chain-runner
  with .orig restore, chain-runner without .orig). E2E worktree
  test adds a regression case for the canonicalize-without-resolve
  fix so a worktree's identity row stays distinct from its repo's.

## Misc

- `tests/test_error_invalid_inputs.py`: a few additional negative
  cases for the new auth helpers (empty token string normalizes
  to None, malformed JSON-RPC payload surfaces as ClickException).
- `tests/test_toon_formatting.py`: trivial assertion update — the
  `resource://projects` rename ripples into one TOON snapshot.
- `tests/test_utils_config_db_edges.py`: cover the new
  `clear_settings_cache` / `_SettingsAccessor` plumbing — calling
  `clear_settings_cache()` between two `get_settings()` reads with
  different env values now sees the second value.

+def _seed_project_agent(project_path: Path, agent_name: str, token: str) -> None:
+    project_key = str(project_path.resolve())
+    slug = f"proj-{hashlib.sha256(project_key.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()[:12]}"
+
+    async def _seed() -> None:
+        await ensure_schema()
+        async with get_session() as session:
+            existing = await session.execute(select(Project).where(cast(Any, Project.human_key == project_key)))
+            project = existing.scalars().first()
+            if project is None:
+                project = Project(slug=slug, human_key=project_key)
+                session.add(project)
+                await session.commit()
+                await session.refresh(project)
+            assert project.id is not None
+            existing_agent = await session.execute(
+                select(Agent).where(
+                    cast(Any, Agent.project_id == project.id),
+                    cast(Any, Agent.name == agent_name),
+                )
+            )
+            agent = existing_agent.scalars().first()
+            if agent is None:
+                session.add(
+                    Agent(
+                        project_id=project.id,
+                        name=agent_name,
+                        program="x",
+                        model="y",
+                        task_description="",
+                        registration_token=token,
+                    )
+                )
+            else:
+                agent.registration_token = token
+            await session.commit()
+
+    asyncio.run(_seed())
+
+

+@pytest.mark.asyncio
+async def test_archive_read_routes_do_not_create_missing_project_archives(isolated_env):
+    """Read-only archive routes must not create projects on disk for nonexistent slugs."""
+    settings = _config.get_settings()
+    server = build_mcp_server()
+    app = build_http_app(settings, server)
+    missing_project_dir = Path(settings.storage.root) / "projects" / "ghost-project"
+
+    transport = ASGITransport(app=app)
+    async with AsyncClient(transport=transport, base_url="http://test") as client:
+        browser_resp = await client.get("/mail/archive/browser", params={"project": "ghost-project"})
+        file_resp = await client.get(
+            "/mail/archive/browser/ghost-project/file",
+            params={"path": "agents/BlueLake/profile.json"},
+        )
+        snapshot_resp = await client.get(
+            "/mail/archive/time-travel/snapshot",
+            params={
+                "project": "ghost-project",
+                "agent": "BlueLake",
+                "timestamp": "2099-12-31T23:59:59Z",
+            },
+        )
+
+    assert browser_resp.status_code == 200
+    assert file_resp.status_code == 404
+    assert snapshot_resp.status_code == 200
+    assert missing_project_dir.exists() is False
+
